data_in.description: example.tsv — tab-delimited metabolomics data file containing columns: aliquot, compound, area, compound_is, area_is, type, injection_time, batch, and concentration

data_out.description: Validation report (CSV or text) confirming that all 'ratio' assay values equal corresponding primary assay values within numerical tolerance (machine epsilon)

domain_knowledge: mzQuality's buildExperiment function auto-generates a 'ratio' assay by dividing the primary assay by a secondary assay (internal standard area); when no secondaryAssay column is supplied, the function must handle this gracefully, typically by setting divisor to 1 or omitting the ratio calculation.
The SummarizedExperiment object stores assays as a list of matrices, each indexed by assay name; verification requires extraction and element-wise comparison.
Tab-delimited format requires exact column names (aliquot, compound, area, type, injection_time, batch) as recognized by readData; missing or misspelled columns will trigger validation failure.

Numerical comparison of floating-point assay values must account for rounding error; exact equality is not expected, but relative difference should be within machine epsilon (~1e-15 for double precision).

direct_checks: verify file 'example.tsv' exists in the mzQuality package repository or deposited dataset
script_runs: execute buildExperiment(example.tsv, primaryAssay='<identified column>', secondaryAssay=NULL) without error in R environment with mzQuality loaded
field_present: verify 'ratio' assay is present in resulting SummarizedExperiment object
output_matches_reference: extract all 'ratio' assay values from output and verify they equal corresponding primary assay values (robust to floating-point precision within machine epsilon)
expert_review: verify that the conditional behaviour documented in the article (divisor defaults to 1 when secondaryAssay is omitted) is correctly implemented and produces mathematically sound ratio calculations
confirm that the ratio assay calculation logic aligns with the stated package design for handling missing secondary assay specifications

evidence_snippets.text: [other] When the secondaryAssay column is not provided to buildExperiment, does the resulting 'ratio' assay equal the primary assay values unchanged?: 'Note that if the `secondaryAssay` column is not provided, its value will be set to `1`, effectively negating the Internal Standard.'

[other] When secondaryAssay is not provided to buildExperiment, its value defaults to 1, which negates the Internal Standard effect, making the ratio assay equal to the primary assay values.: 'Note that if the `secondaryAssay` column is not provided, its value will be set to `1`, effectively negating the Internal Standard.'

[other] example.tsv — tab-delimited metabolomics data file containing columns: aliquot, compound, area, compound_is, area_is, type, injection_time, batch, and concentration: 'The data should be in a tab-delimited format containing at least the following columns: aliquot, compound, area, type, injection_time, batch.'

[other] Validation report (CSV or text) confirming that all 'ratio' assay values equal corresponding primary assay values within numerical tolerance (machine epsilon): 'The `buildExperiment` function will then take the data and create an experiment object that can be used for analysis.'
[other] mzQuality: 'mzQuality requires a specific format for the input data.'
[intro] SummarizedExperiment: 'Internally, mzQuality uses Bioconductors' *SummarizedExperiment* object to store the data.'

cmd: library(mzQuality); library(SummarizedExperiment); data <- readData('{inputs.example_tsv}'); se <- buildExperiment(data); ratio_assay <- assay(se, 'ratio'); primary_assay <- assay(se, 1); all_equal <- all(abs(ratio_assay - primary_assay) < 1e-14 * pmax(abs(primary_assay), 1)); report <- data.frame(test='ratio_equals_primary', passed=all_equal, tolerance=1e-14); write.csv(report, 'validation_report.csv', row.names=FALSE); cat('Validation complete. All ratio values equal primary assay:', all_equal, '\n')
executable.grounding_notes: Unverified by the executable guard — command runs inline code (-c/-e); its API calls cannot be validated against package files. Treat this command as a best-effort inference; verify the entry point and APIs against the real code before running.

expected_outputs: Validation report (CSV or text) confirming that all 'ratio' assay values equal corresponding primary assay values within numerical tolerance (machine epsilon)
finding: When secondaryAssay is not provided to buildExperiment, its value defaults to 1, which negates the Internal Standard effect, making the ratio assay equal to the primary assay values.
inputs: example.tsv — tab-delimited metabolomics data file containing columns: aliquot, compound, area, compound_is, area_is, type, injection_time, batch, and concentration
methodology_summary: Load example.tsv using readData function to validate column structure and data integrity.
Construct SummarizedExperiment object via buildExperiment without secondaryAssay parameter.
Extract 'ratio' and primary assays from the experiment object.
Perform element-wise numerical comparison of ratio assay to primary assay values.
Validation: all ratio values must equal primary assay values within double-precision floating-point tolerance (relative difference < 1e-14).
research_question: When the secondaryAssay column is not provided to buildExperiment, does the resulting 'ratio' assay equal the primary assay values unchanged?

task_description: Load a tab-delimited metabolomics data file (example.tsv) using readData, construct a SummarizedExperiment object via buildExperiment without the secondaryAssay column, and verify that the resulting 'ratio' assay entries equal the raw primary assay values (divisor = 1 throughout).
task_objective: Verify that buildExperiment without secondaryAssay produces ratio assay values equal to primary assay values by confirming the effective divisor is 1.
title: Extend mzQuality to support a no-internal-standard condition and verify ratio assay defaults to 1

Synthesis grounding: the following tools/outputs were NOT found in the source paper and are inferred — verify before use: SummarizedExperiment, Validation report (CSV or text) confirming that all 'ratio' assay values equal corresponding primary assay values within numerical tolerance (machine epsilon).
workflow_description: 1. Load the example.tsv file from the mzQuality package using readData function, which performs basic checks on column integrity. 2. Call buildExperiment on the resulting data frame without specifying a secondaryAssay column to generate a SummarizedExperiment object. 3. Extract the 'ratio' assay from the experiment object and compare element-wise to the primary assay values to verify identity (effective divisor of 1). 4. Generate a validation report confirming equality across all compound–sample pairs.
